title: Stakeholder Task, Workflow Fit, Authority, Risk, and Evidence · Runtime Lens: Harm Risk Tier
summary: A sponsor asks for an autonomous assistant but has not defined the user decision, accepted failure, permitted actions, affected people, oversight, or why a deterministic workflow is insufficient. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the harm risk tier increment in bounded agent product and risk charter. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.
Compare deterministic code, workflow, model-assisted step, and agent loop and choose the least variable architecture that satisfies the task.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-product-risk-contract-2
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-product-risk-contract-2.
# Case 7417: a multilingual case-routing evaluation.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter.
def worked_agentpy_product_risk_contract_2(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, risk_controls: int = 6, unresolved_high_risks: int = 0, escalation_paths: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorityworked2",
        "interaction7417",
        "tool2dekrow",
        "stateworked27417",
        "evaluation7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authority-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if risk_controls < 4 or unresolved_high_risks > 0:
        return False, risk_controls, "risk-uncontrolled"
    if escalation_paths < 2:
        return False, escalation_paths, "escalation-missing"
    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4 and unresolved_high_risks == 0
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-agent-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python agent decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected model, interaction, schema, tool, authority, state, approval, memory, evaluation, security, telemetry, sandbox, deployment, or recovery case, then state which provider, Gemini, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, identity, storage, browser, accessibility, privacy, load, cost, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Build Harm Risk Tier evidence · Theory Harm Risk Tier
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-harm-risk-tier-1h0utx" that uses case 3343 to gate agent construction on stakeholder value, workflow fit, authority, measurable failure, risk controls, and escalation. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-harm-risk-tier-1h0utx
# Competency: agentpy-harm-risk-tier.

def evidence_agentpy_harm_risk_tier_1h0utx(authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 8, risk_controls: int = 6, unresolved_high_risks: int = 0, escalation_paths: int = 4) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

    evidence_axes_1h0utx = (
        "authority1h0utx",
        "interaction3343",
        "toolxtu0h1",
        "state1h0utx3343",
        "evaluation33431h0utx",
        "recoveryxtu0h13343",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1h0utx)) == 6
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "authority-missing"
    if acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "outcome-underdefined"
    if risk_controls < 4 or unresolved_high_risks > 0:

    if escalation_paths < 2:

    assert authorized and acceptance_cases >= 4 and unresolved_high_risks == 0
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-agent-charter"

At Safe AI agents and Python Repair the Harm Risk Tier defect · Theory Harm Risk Tier, trace this concrete input and predict model request, interaction step, schema validation, tool choice, authorization, approval, result, state transition, memory, evaluation, trace, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and controlled-provider evidence before reviewing it: A theory agent engineering team handles case 5587 during a multilingual case-routing evaluation. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate agent construction on stakeholder value, workflow fit, authority, measurable failure, risk controls, and escalation; loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets; then replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. stimulus.title: Harm Risk Tier failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Risk depends only on whether the model can generate harmful words.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.

options.text: For repair the harm risk tier defect, run one convenient fluent model response, omit stakeholder risk, revision, model and API identity, interaction and call correlation, tool schema and authority, state and resource budgets, approval, changed fixtures, injection, privacy, accessibility, evaluation, recovery, and controlled provider transfer, then accept plausible text as agent proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Harm Risk Tier, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the harm risk tier defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Harm Risk Tier model · Theory Harm Risk Tier
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Harm Risk Tier: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory agent engineering team handles case 3968 during a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate agent construction on stakeholder value, workflow fit, authority, measurable failure, risk controls, and escalation; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Harm Risk Tier model · Theory Harm Risk Tier.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Safe AI agents and Python Defend the Harm Risk Tier model · Theory Harm Risk Tier, keep Harm Risk Tier connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify impact, reversibility, affected groups, uncertainty, data sensitivity, action authority, and required human oversight.
